items.title: CMA recommends overhaul of road and rail procurement to cut costs and drive economic growth through infrastructure investment
items.snippet: Civil engineering market study sets out recommendations to tackle long-standing delivery issues in road and rail infrastructure.

items.title: Commission approves €1.3 billion German State aid to support renewable hydrogen production
items.snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.3 billion German State aid scheme to support the production of renewable hydrogen through the European Hydrogen Bank's “Auctions-as-a-Service” tool for the auction that closed in 2026.

items.title: Commission approves €288 million German State aid for first-of-a-kind facilities in semiconductor value chain
items.snippet: The European Commission has approved €288 million in German State aid to support the setting up two new facilities in the semiconductor supply chain.

items.title: CMA conditionally clears Getty merger with Shutterstock
items.snippet: CMA independent inquiry group concludes deal can proceed if Shutterstock sells its editorial business

items.title: CMA launches strategic market status investigation into Microsoft’s business software ecosystem
items.snippet: CMA sets out scope of its investigation to determine whether Microsoft should be designated with strategic market status for its business software ecosystem.

items.title: Commission approves €1.5 billion Spanish State aid scheme to support farmers affected by 2025-2026 floods in Andalusia and Extremadura
items.snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.5 billion Spanish scheme to support farmers affected by a series of adverse meteorological events in the regions of Andalusia and Extremadura between 10 November 2025 and 9 February 2026.

items.title: Commission invites comments on draft revised State aid rules for air transport
items.snippet: The European Commission has today launched a public consultation inviting all interested parties to comment on its draft revised Guidelines on State aid to the air transport sector. These will replace the existing Guidelines, which were adopted in 2014.

items.title: Commission unconditionally approves Suzano's acquisition of Kimberly-Clark IFP
items.snippet: The European Commission has unconditionally approved, under the EU Merger Regulation, the proposed acquisition by Suzano S.A. ('Suzano') of Kimberly Clark IFP NewCo B.V. ('Kimberly-Clark IFP').

items.title: Commission approves €5 billion German State aid scheme to support decarbonisation of industry
items.snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €5 billion German scheme to help companies in industrial sectors decarbonise their production processes.

items.title: NEW! Commission publishes 2025 Report on competition policy
items.snippet: Press Release - NEW! Commission publishes 2025 Report on competition policy

items.title: Commission approves over €800 million in State aid to Lithuanian development bank
items.snippet: Commission approves over €800 million in State aid to Lithuanian development bank

items.title: CMA publishes latest monitoring report on road fuel market
items.snippet: Report assesses impact of the conflict in the Middle East on fuel prices and margins until the end of March 2026.

items.title: Commission opens consultation on draft of new Merger Guidelines
items.snippet: Commission opens consultation on draft of new Merger Guidelines

items.title: Commission adopts temporary State aid framework to support sectors affected by Middle East crisis
items.snippet: Commission adopts temporary State aid framework to support sectors affected by Middle East crisis

items.title: Review of Vandemoortele / Délifrance deal moves to in-depth investigation
items.snippet: Referral to phase 2 comes after period to resolve concerns has expired.

items.title: CMA appoints new non-executive directors
items.snippet: CMA welcomes newly appointed non-executive directors to the board.

items.title: CMA orders the AA and BSM driving schools to refund learner drivers over drip pricing
items.snippet: The AA Driving School and BSM Driving School – both of which are owned by the AA – must refund more than 80,000 customers and pay a fine of £4.2 million.

items.title: CMA announces package of actions on business software and cloud services
items.snippet: CMA aims to create greater choice for UK businesses and public sector at pivotal time of advances in AI.

items.title: Business software and cloud services
items.snippet: Actions set out by the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) on business software and cloud services.

items.title: Fake and misleading reviews: 5 businesses under CMA investigation
items.snippet: Five companies now under investigation as the CMA steps up its work to tackle fake reviews and misleading star ratings – and protect people online.

The Antimonopoly Office of the Slovak Republic Imposed a Fine Exceeding EUR 675,000 for the Abuse of Dominance
